title: Brigadeiro de Colher
description: My Thermomix version of Rita Lobo's spoonable brigadeiro — soft, glossy Brazilian chocolate fudge you eat with a spoon.
intro: Rita Lobo taught a generation of Brazilians to cook, and her 'brigadeiro de colher' is pure comfort — softer than the rolled kind, scooped warm from the pot. The Thermomix stirs it for you, so it never catches or burns.
steps: Place condensed milk, cocoa, butter, cream and salt in the mixing bowl. Mix 10 sec / 0°C / speed 4 until the cocoa is fully combined.
Cook 12 min / 100°C / speed 2, MC off with the simmering basket on the lid, until thick and glossy.
Scrape down and cook 2 min / Varoma / speed 2, MC off, for a deeper spoonable brigadeiro texture.
Spoon into little glasses 1 min / 0°C / no speed, scatter with chocolate sprinkles and serve warm or chilled.
tips: The 'de colher' style is meant to be soft — stop a minute or two earlier than you would for rolled brigadeiro.
Add a spoon of cream after cooking for an even silkier texture.
Use good cocoa — it's the main flavour here.

faq.a: It's the spoonable version of Brazil's famous brigadeiro — cooked a touch less so it stays soft and creamy, served in a glass and eaten with a spoon rather than rolled into balls.

faq.q: Why cook it in the Thermomix?
faq.a: At speed 2 the blade stirs constantly, so the brigadeiro cooks evenly and never catches on the base — no standing over a hot pot.

faq.q: Is this the original Rita Lobo recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Rita Lobo and brigadeiro de colher; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
